mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable model serving. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware configurations and potential complexity in setup for distributed environments.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository for version 0.18.0.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
This step installs the necessary CRDs for vLLM to function correctly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs required for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application in a specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource to deploy a model with vLLM.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This command creates an example vLLM resource for the GPT-3 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the deployed vLLM resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> This command lists the vLLM resources in the 'vllm' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all related data and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Backup the current vLLM configuration.
> This command upgrades vLLM to version 0.18.0.
> Verify that the vLLM resource is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the vLLM pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the model server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The model server may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Ensure the model is correctly specified in the Custom Resource and check the pod status.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for vLLM.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the vLLM Custom Resource or scale your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions may not have been installed.

**Fix:**
Re-run the CRD installation command: 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved performance for existing models.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM resource listed with a status of 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and that you have the necessary tools installed.
